texto: Writers more interested in literature than the truth ensure that they never come out with either thing — one reason that the word literature today sounds so fake, as if you were to insist on saying cuisine every time you meant food. Food, as in sustenance, is more like what we have in mind.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a The editors n+1: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. O trecho “Writers more interested in literature than the truth ensure that they never come out with either …” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que tempo, contemporary-literature, literature pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de literature e writing.

frase_original: Writers more interested in literature than the truth ensure that they never come out with either thing — one reason that the word literature today sounds so fake, as if you were to insist on saying cuisine every time you meant food. Food, as in sustenance, is more like what we have in mind.
